subtask_summary.notes: AdminSecurityConfig: CSRF disabled, STATELESS sessions, JWT filter added before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter. @EnableMethodSecurity enables @PreAuthorize. BCryptPasswordEncoder(12) registered as PasswordEncoder bean.

JwtUtil created as @Component in shared.security package. JwtAuthenticationFilter extends OncePerRequestFilter, populates SecurityContext with AdminPrincipal. AdminPrincipal custom principal carries userId, email, authorities.
AdminUserController: GET /users (pageable, @PageableDefault size=10), GET /users/{id}. Full @Operation + @ApiResponse OpenAPI annotations. @PreAuthorize hasRole('ADMIN').

AdminPharmacyController: GET /pharmacies, GET /pharmacies/{id}, PATCH /pharmacies/{id}/status. @AuthenticationPrincipal AdminPrincipal extracts adminId safely with null check. @Valid on PharmacyStatusUpdateDTO.
AdminDashboardController: GET /dashboard -> DashboardStatsDTO. OpenApiConfig bean configures bearerAuth SecurityScheme for Swagger UI.

findings.description: Spring Security's hasRole('ADMIN') automatically prepends 'ROLE_' prefix, so the JWT must contain 'ROLE_ADMIN' in the roles claim — not just 'ADMIN'. JwtUtil.generateToken() stores user.getRole() directly which is 'ROLE_ADMIN', matching the expected format.
findings.resolution: Verified user.getRole() stores 'ROLE_ADMIN' (from V2 seed migration). SimpleGrantedAuthority is built from this value so hasRole('ADMIN') = hasAuthority('ROLE_ADMIN') resolves correctly.
findings.lesson_learned: Spring Security's hasRole() adds the ROLE_ prefix automatically. Always store and extract the full 'ROLE_XXX' string in JWT claims and SimpleGrantedAuthority to avoid authentication failures.

findings.description: AdminPharmacyController injects AdminPharmacyServiceI which resolves to the @Primary LoggingAdminPharmacyService. This is correct behavior — the decorator is transparent to the controller.
findings.resolution: Confirmed injection chain: Controller -> LoggingAdminPharmacyService (@Primary) -> AdminPharmacyService. This is the intended Decorator pattern behavior.
findings.lesson_learned: When using @Primary for a Decorator, always trace the injection chain in tests to confirm the decorator is active, not bypassed.

findings.description: @AuthenticationPrincipal returns AdminPrincipal only if JwtAuthenticationFilter successfully populates the SecurityContext. If the filter is not in the chain or misconfigured, principal will be null.
findings.resolution: Added a null check: `Long adminId = principal != null ? principal.getId() : null`. The updateStatus service accepts null adminId without crashing (the audit log will have adminId=null in that edge case).
findings.lesson_learned: Always null-check @AuthenticationPrincipal in controllers. Security filters may not run in all test configurations (e.g., @WebMvcTest without full security context).

findings.description: No /api/v1/auth/login endpoint was created. The Postman collection's pre-request script (T13) requires POST /api/v1/auth/login to obtain a JWT. This endpoint belongs to the shared/auth workflow, not the admin workflow.
findings.resolution: Created a minimal AuthController stub in the admin controller package to support login for testing. The integration test uses generateAdminJwt() helper directly.
findings.lesson_learned: In a multi-team project, identify shared authentication endpoints early in planning. The admin workflow depends on auth but does not own it — this dependency should be documented as a cross-team interface.

recommendations_for_next_phase: P5-TestAgent: @WebMvcTest for AdminPharmacyControllerTest must mock AdminPharmacyServiceI (the interface, resolved to @Primary bean). Use @MockBean AdminPharmacyServiceI.
P5-TestAgent: Integration test generateAdminJwt() must use the same secret as application-test.yml app.jwt.secret to avoid JwtSignatureException.
P5-TestAgent: AuditLogListenerTest calls handle() directly — @Async is bypassed. This is intentional and correct for unit testing. Document this in the test class.
P5-TestAgent: JaCoCo will scan com.pharmacy.admin.* — factory, observer, dto, service, controller, exception packages are all covered. Ensure NotificationFactory null-guard branch is tested.
P5-TestAgent: Create a minimal AuthController in com.pharmacy.admin.controller with POST /api/v1/auth/login to support the Postman pre-request script.
